title: Chyme sampling from the digestive tract of Octopus maya
author: Daisy Pineda-Suazo

steps.title: Ensure all tools and equipment are...
steps.instruction: Ensure all tools and equipment are clean and sterilized before starting.

steps.title: Prepare the workspace with necessary materials,...
steps.instruction: Prepare the workspace with necessary materials, ensuring easy access to surgical instruments, centrifuge, liquid nitrogen, and Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Use proper personal protective equipment (PPE) throughout the procedure
steps.instruction: Use proper personal protective equipment (PPE) throughout the procedure.

steps.title: Carefully dissect the digestive tract of...
steps.instruction: Carefully dissect the digestive tract of Octopus maya, ensuring the integrity of each section.

steps.title: Identify and separate the following sections
steps.instruction: Identify and separate the following sections: Anterior salivary glands (BM) Crop (Cr) Stomach (St) Caecum (Ca) Digestive Gland (DG)

steps.title: Use surgical clamps to isolate and...
steps.instruction: Use surgical clamps to isolate and prevent mixing of digestive contents between sections.

steps.title: Open each section carefully to access the chyme
steps.instruction: Open each section carefully to access the chyme.

steps.title: Transfer the chyme into labeled Eppendorf tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the chyme into labeled Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge the chyme samples at 13370...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the chyme samples at 13370 x g, °C, 5 minutes to separate solids from the supernatant.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant and immediately freeze it in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant and immediately freeze it in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Store the frozen samples at -40 °C until analysis
steps.instruction: Store the frozen samples at -40 °C until analysis.

steps.title: Liquefy and homogenize the glands using a tissue homogenizer (e
steps.instruction: Liquefy and homogenize the glands using a tissue homogenizer (e.g., ULTRA-TURRAX IKA T18 basic) at an appropriate speed for uniform homogenization.

steps.title: Transfer the homogenized sample into an Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenized sample into an Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 13370 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 13370 x g, °C, 5 minutes to obtain a clear supernatant.

steps.title: Freeze the supernatant in liquid nitrogen immediately after centrifugation
steps.instruction: Freeze the supernatant in liquid nitrogen immediately after centrifugation.

steps.title: Store the sample at -40 °C until further analysis
steps.instruction: Store the sample at -40 °C until further analysis. Notes and precautions: Ensure the surgical clamps are properly placed to prevent cross-contamination of digestive contents. Work quickly when handling biological samples to preserve enzyme activity. Properly label all tubes with the section of the digestive tract and the sample type for accurate identification. Use liquid nitrogen with caution and follow appropriate safety protocols to avoid burns or injuries. Storage: Samples should remain frozen at -40 °C to preserve enzyme and metabolite integrity until the time of analysis.
